title: Velvety Light Malaysian Clotted Cream Panna Cotta
description: This dessert combines the rich flavors of clotted cream with a light and creamy texture, ideal for those watching their sugar intake.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup full-fat coconut milk
ingredients.notes: full fat for better texture and flavor

ingredients.name: 3 tablespoons granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: for those watching their carbs, use stevia or monk fruit sweetener instead

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: use at room temperature for better spreading and distribution

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons cornstarch
ingredients.notes: for thickening the base without adding excess sugar or fat

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup clotted cream (from cans, not made at home)

instructions: Combine the heavy whipping cream, coconut milk, sugar, butter, cornstarch, and salt in a medium saucepan. Heat over medium-high heat until it starts to boil.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and stir constantly with a whisk until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool for at least 10-15 minutes.
Gently fold in the clotted cream using a rubber spatula. The mixture will be very thin but should hold together when folded gently.
Pour into individual serving dishes or ramekins. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours or until firm.
Serve cold with additional clotted cream on top if desired.
